content.problemStatement:
An **ordered pair** (A,B)(A,B)(A,B) is said to be valid iff max‚Å°(A,B)=k‚ãÖmin‚Å°(A,B)\max(A,B) = k\cdot \min(A,B)max(A,B)=k‚ãÖmin(A,B), where k>1k \gt 1k>1.

Let S(N)S(N)S(N) denote the set of all valid pairs (A,B)(A,B)(A,B) where (1‚â§A,B‚â§N)(1 \leq A,B \leq N)(1‚â§A,B‚â§N).

Carol chooses a valid pair (A,B)(A,B)(A,B) from S(N)S(N)S(N) **randomly** and plays `Guess Game` with Alice and Bob.

She will tell:


The value AAA to Alice;
The value BBB to Bob;
The condition (A,B)(A,B)(A,B) is a valid pair to **both** Alice and Bob.
Note\textbf{Note}Note: She will not\textbf{not}not tell the value of NNN to either Alice or Bob.

The guessing starts and both players take turns making guesses for the value min‚Å°(A,B)\min(A,B)min(A,B), with Alice starting **first**.

In their turn, a player can do one of the following two things:


Say `"I don‚Äôt know"`, and pass the turn to other player;
Say `"I know,` min‚Å°(A,B)\min(A,B)min(A,B) `is` ‚Ä¶\dots‚Ä¶ `"`.

Note that both Alice and Bob are smart enough and only guess the value when they are completely sure. Moreover, the game **ends** once any of them guesses the value.
Please read sample explanation for elaborated guessing process.

For a given NNN, let XXX denote the **total** number of **turns** taken by both players in the game. Calculate the mathematical expectation of XXX.

The value can be represented as an irreducible fraction PQ\frac{P}{Q}QP‚Äã, where PPP and QQQ are coprime integers. You need to output P‚ãÖQ‚àí1P \cdot Q^{-1}P‚ãÖQ‚àí1 modulo (109+7)(10^9+7)(109+7), where Q‚àí1Q^{-1}Q‚àí1 represents the multiplicative inverse of QQQ modulo (109+7)(10^9+7)(109+7).


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists a single integer NNN.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the mathematical expectation of XXX.


The value can be represented as an irreducible fraction PQ\frac{P}{Q}QP‚Äã, where PPP and QQQ are coprime integers. You need to output P‚ãÖQ‚àí1P \cdot Q^{-1}P‚ãÖQ‚àí1 modulo (109+7)(10^9+7)(109+7), where Q‚àí1Q^{-1}Q‚àí1 represents the multiplicative inverse of QQQ modulo (109+7)(10^9+7)(109+7).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: There are 888 valid pairs: (1,2),(1,3),(1,4),(2,4),(2,1),(3,1),(4,1),(4,2)(1,2),(1,3),(1,4),(2,4),(2,1),(3,1),(4,1),(4,2)(1,2),(1,3),(1,4),(2,4),(2,1),(3,1),(4,1),(4,2).
For the valid pair (2,4)(2,4)(2,4), the guessing process is as follows:

Turn 111: Alice says "I don‚Äôt know"
Turn 222: Bob says "I don‚Äôt know"
Turn 333: Alice says "I know, min‚Å°(A,B)\min(A,B)min(A,B) is 2". The game ends.
In this case, the value of X=3X = 3X=3 as they took a total of 333 turns.

The mathematical expectation of XXX is 1+1+1+3+2+2+2+28=74=750000007(mod‚Äâ‚Äâ109+7)\frac{1+1+1+3+2+2+2+2}{8}=\frac{7}{4}=750000007(\mod 10^9+7)81+1+1+3+2+2+2+2‚Äã=47‚Äã=750000007(mod109+7)
